danger_signals.visual_form: Various animated animals and prehistoric creatures
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile entities that damage player on contact; require club combat to neutralize

danger_signals.visual_form: Humorous end-of-level boss characters with distinct visual designs
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring skill-based combat; marks level completion checkpoint

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental drop-offs and obstacles (inferred from platformer genre)
danger_signals.meaning: Instant death or damage zones; require precise jumping to avoid

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual food objects scattered throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Healing/sustenance; provides progression momentum and points

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinctive lighter object placed in levels
safety_signals.meaning: Essential item required to unlock next level; marks safe checkpoint

safety_signals.visual_form: Breakable walls and spots revealable by club strikes
safety_signals.meaning: Safe exploration zones containing bonus food and collectibles; reward curiosity

safety_signals.visual_form: Alternative level pathways (3 bonus levels total)
safety_signals.meaning: Optional safe content zones offering extra points and secrets

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character animation shows caveman breathing heavily, out of breath
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must stop running; movement speed reduced until rest; communicates resource management mechanic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lighter item appears or becomes accessible in level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level exit becomes available; triggers advancement to next stage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stone Age setting with animals, primitive environment, caveman protagonist with club
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Core gameplay loop: hunt/forage for food, combat animals with melee weapon

environmental_storytelling.example: Food items and diamonds scattered throughout environment communicate world as resource-scarcity setting; player must actively forage
Video games and modern items available as collectibles in prehistoric setting; creates tonal contrast and easter egg rewards
Extra credits and holiday photographs of development team hidden in breakable areas; rewards thorough exploration with meta-narrative
Platformer obstacles (pits, enemies) distributed across ten main levels plus three bonus stages; communicates progressive difficulty and expanded optional content

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Out of breath animation plays; movement halts temporarily; communicates need for rest/pacing
Patrol or attack; can be defeated by club strikes; respawn behavior inferred from standard platformer design
Unique combat pattern; humorous design suggests satirical difficulty; defeat triggers level completion

notes: Prehistorik 2 uses minimal visual communication systems typical of early 1990s platformers. World-state manifestation relies primarily on: (1) character animation for fatigue/status, (2) collectible item placement for progression gating, (3) enemy presence for danger signaling, (4) environmental layout for hazard communication. No documented faction systems or political themes exist in this game—it operates as a pure action-platformer with prehistoric survival framing. Humor and anachronism drive environmental storytelling alongside resource scarcity mechanics.
